Question: A and B in the following reactions are

Statement I: In a dorsiventral leaf, the abaxial epidermis may even lack stomata
Statement II: The tissue between the upper and lower epidermis is called the mesophyll; it possesses chloroplasts and carry out photosynthesis, is made up of collenchyma
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: In a dorsiventral leaf, the adaxial epidermis may even lack stomata
Statement II is incorrect: The tissue between the upper and lower epidermis is called the mesophyll; it possesses chloroplasts and carry out photosynthesis, is made up of parenchyma

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: Open vascular bundles possess cambium and the ability to form secondary tissues
Statement II: Monocotyledons possess open vascular bundles because they lack cambium
Statement III: Radial vascular bundles are those where xylem and phloem are arranged alternately along different radii
Statement IV: Conjoint vascular bundles are common in stems and leaves
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Monocotyledons are referred to as closed because they have no cambium

Question:
Statement I: Dicot stems have closed vascular bundles because they lack cambium
Statement II: Monocot stems have open vascular bundles because they possess cambium
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both are incorrect. Dicot stems have open vascular bundles because they possess cambium. Monocot stems have closed bundles because they lack cambium

Question:
Statement I: The tangential and radial walls of the endodermal cells in roots have a deposition of suberin called Casparian strips
Statement II: Suberin is a water-permeable, waxy material
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Suberin is water-impermeable

Question:
Statement I: In conjoint vascular bundles, xylem and phloem are arranged in an alternate manner along different radii
Statement II: Conjoint vascular bundles are common in stems and leaves
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Conjoint bundles have xylem and phloem situated along the same radius. The alternate arrangement on different radii is called radial

Question:
Match the tissue in Column I with its location in a dorsiventral leaf in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Adaxial epidermis(i)Vertically arranged elongated cells
B.Abaxial epidermis(ii)Below palisade reaching lower epidermis
C.Palisade parenchyma(iii)Upper surface
D.Spongy parenchyma(iv)Bears more stomata
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iii, B-iv, C-i, D-ii

Question:
Assertion (A): In dicotyledonous stems, the vascular bundles are scattered throughout the ground tissue
Reason (R): The "ring" arrangement of vascular bundles is a characteristic feature of the dicot stem
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is false: Dicot stems have a ring arrangement ; scattered bundles are found in monocot stems.

Question:
Match the feature in Column I with its effect in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Bulliform cells (turgid)(i)Near similar sizes of vascular bundles
B.Bulliform cells (flaccid)(ii)Stomata on both surfaces
C.Isobilateral leaf(iii)Leaves curl inwards
D.Parallel venation(iv)Leaf surface exposed
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i

Question:
Statement I: The phloem parenchyma is present in the vascular bundles of a monocot stem
Statement II: Water-containing cavities are present within the vascular bundles of a monocot stem
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Phloem parenchyma is absent in monocot stems. Statement II is correct

Question:
Assertion (A): Grasses have isobilateral leaves where the mesophyll is differentiated into palisade and spongy parenchyma
Reason (R): In an isobilateral leaf, stomata are present on both surfaces of the epidermis
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is false: In an isobilateral leaf, the mesophyll is not differentiated. (R) is true as stomata are on both surfaces in monocots.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: Ground tissue consists of complex tissues such as parenchyma, collenchyma, and sclerenchyma
Statement II: Parenchymatous cells are usually present in the cortex, pericycle, pith, and medullary rays
Statement III: In leaves, the ground tissue consists of thick-walled cells called mesophyll
Statement IV: All tissues except epidermis and vascular bundles constitute the ground tissue
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Ground tissue consists of simple tissues such as parenchyma, collenchyma, and sclerenchyma
Statement III is incorrect: Mesophyll is made of thin-walled chloroplast-containing cells.

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Dicot stems have collenchymatous hypodermis just below the epidermis
Statement II: The endodermis of the dicot stem is also referred to as the starch sheath
Statement III: Pericycle in dicot stems is found as semi-lunar patches of sclerenchyma above the phloem
Statement IV: Dicot stems have scattered vascular bundles throughout the ground tissue
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Dicot stems have a ring arrangement of vascular bundles. Scattered bundles are found in monocot stems.

Question:
Statement I: In the dicotyledonous stem, vascular bundles are arranged in a ring
Statement II: Vascular bundles in the monocotyledonous stem are also arranged in a ring
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Monocot stems have a large number of scattered vascular bundles.

Question:
Statement I: Roots show a radial arrangement of vascular bundles
Statement II: Monocotyledonous roots do not undergo any secondary growth
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Assertion (A): The epidermis which covers both the upper surface (adaxial epidermis) and lower surface (abaxial epidermis) of the leaf has a conspicuous cuticle
Reason (R): The tissue between the upper and lower epidermis is called the mesophyll and numerous large spaces and air cavities are between these cells.
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true. However, the definition of mesophyll (R) does not explain the distribution of cuticle on the leaf surfaces (A).

Question:
Statement I: Trichomes are unicellular elongations of epidermal cells found on the root
Statement II: Root hairs help in preventing water loss due to transpiration
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both statements are incorrect. Trichomes are found on the stem and are usually multicellular. It is trichomes (not root hairs) that help prevent water loss from transpiration; root hairs are for absorbing water and minerals.

Question:
Match the structure in Column I with its specific feature in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Epiblema(i)Two to four patches
B.Casparian strips(ii)Suberin deposition
C.Xylem/Phloem(iii)Small or inconspicuous
D.Pith(iv)Outermost layer
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iv, B-ii, C-i, D-iii

Question:
Assertion (A): In the leaves of grasses, the guard cells are dumb-bell shaped
Reason (R): Guard cells possess chloroplasts and playa vital role in regulating the opening and closing of stomata
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true. However, the presence of chloroplasts or general regulation (R) does not explain the specific dumb-bell shape (A) found in grasses

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: In Dicot roots, next to endodermis lies a few layers of thin-walled parenchymatous cells referred to as pericycle
Statement II: Monocot roots usually have more than six (polyarch) xylem bundles
Statement III: In dicot root, many of the cells of epiblema protrude in the form of trichomes
Statement IV: Monocotyledonous roots do not undergo any secondary growth
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement I: In Dicot roots, next to endodermis lies a few layers of thick-walled parenchymatous cells referred to as pericycle
Statement III: In dicot root, many of the cells of epiblema protrude in the form of unicellular root hairs

Question:
Match the feature in Column I with the correct root type in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Small pith(i)Monocotyledonous root
B.Large pith(ii)Dicot root lateral initiation
C.Pericycle role(iii)Dicotyledonous root
D.Secondary growth(iv)Absent in monocot roots
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iii, B-i, C-ii, D-iv

Question:
Assertion (A): The vascular bundles in a monocotyledonous stem are described as "closed"
Reason (R): Monocotyledonous stems do not possess cambium in their vascular bundles and thus do not form secondary tissues
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both are true. The absence of cambium (R) is the reason why these bundles are classified as closed (A).

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Root hairs are multicellular elongations of epidermal cells that help absorb water
Statement II: Trichomes in the shoot system are usually multicellular and may be branched or unbranched
Statement III: Trichomes help in preventing water loss due to transpiration
Statement IV: Trichomes may be secretory in nature
Options: Statements II and III are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect. Root hairs are specifically described as unicellular elongations.

Question:
Match the arrangement in Column I with its anatomical location in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Radial(i)Dicotyledonous stems
B.Closed(ii)Stems and leaves
C.Open(iii)Roots
D.Conjoint(iv)Monocotyledons
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iii, B-iv, C-i, D-ii

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: In grasses, the guard cells are dumb-bell shaped
Statement II: The inner walls of guard cells, towards the stomatal pore, are thin, while the outer walls are highly thickened
Statement III: Guard cells possess chloroplasts and regulate the opening and closing of stomata
Statement IV: Specialized epidermal cells in the vicinity of guard cells are known as subsidiary cells
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: The outer walls are thin and the inner walls (towards the pore) are highly thickened.

Question:
Match the tissue region in Column I with its description in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Endodermis(i)Innermost layer of cortex
B.Pericycle(ii)Parenchyma between xylem and phloem
C.Pith(iii)Thick-walled cells inside endodermis
D.Conjunctive tissue(iv)Central portion of the primary organ
Solution: The correct matching is: A-i, B-iii, C-iv, D-ii

Question:
Match the system in Column I with its components in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Epidermal(i)Cortex, pericycle, pith
B.Ground(ii)Xylem and phloem
C.Vascular(iii)Trichomes and hairs
D.Mesophyll(iv)Ground tissue of leaf
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iii, B-i, C-ii, D-iv

Question:
Assertion (A): The cuticle is a waxy thick layer that helps in preventing the loss of water from the plant body
Reason (R): The cuticle is found on the epidermis of leaves and roots to facilitate better water management
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is false: The cuticle is explicitly absent in roots.

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Bulliform cells are large, empty and colourless cells found in grasses
Statement II: When bulliform cells are turgid, the leaf surface is exposed
Statement III: When flaccid due to water stress, bulliform cells make leaves curl inwards to minimize water loss
Statement IV: Bulliform cells are modified abaxial epidermal cells
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Bulliform cells are modifications of certain adaxial (upper) epidermal cells

Question:
Statement I: In grasses, the guard cells of the stomata are bean-shaped
Statement II: Stomata are structures present in the epidermis of roots that regulate transpiration
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both statements are incorrect. In grasses, guard cells are dumb-bell shaped. Stomata are present in the epidermis of leaves, and the cuticle (not stomata) is absent in roots.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer
Statement I: The outermost layer of a dicot root is the epiblema
Statement II: The endodermis comprises a single layer of barrel-shaped cells with intercellular spaces
Statement III: Casparian strips are water-impermeable waxy depositions of cuticle
Statement IV: Initiation of lateral roots takes place in the pericycle cells
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Endodermal cells are without any intercellular spaces
Statement III is incorrect: Casparian strips are water-impermeable waxy depositions of suberin

Question:
Statement I: The epidermal tissue system forms the outermost covering of the whole plant body
Statement II: The ground tissue system is divided into three zones – cortex, pericycle, and pith
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Match the cortical zone in Column I with its function/nature in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Hypodermis(i)Rich in starch grains
B.Cortical layers(ii)Mechanical strength to young stem
C.Endodermis(iii)Rounded cells with intercellular spaces
D.Pericycle(iv)Semi-lunar patches of sclerenchyma
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv; Dicot hypodermis provides strength. General cortex has spaces. Endodermis stores starch. Pericycle is sclerenchymatous

Question:
Assertion (A): The pith in a monocotyledonous root is small and inconspicuous
Reason (R): Monocotyledonous roots usually have more than six xylem bundles, a condition known as polyarch
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is false: The pith in monocot roots is large and well developed. (R) is true as monocot roots are polyarch.

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: In a dorsiventral leaf, the abaxial epidermis generally bears more stomata than the adaxial epidermis
Statement II: Palisade parenchyma is placed abaxially and is made of elongated cells
Statement III: Spongy parenchyma is situated below the palisade cells and extends to the lower epidermis
Statement IV: Size of vascular bundles in dicot leaves depends on the size of the veins
Options: Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Palisade parenchyma is placed adaxially (upper surface).

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Monocot stems have a sclerenchymatous hypodermis
Statement II: Each vascular bundle in a monocot stem is surrounded by a sclerenchymatous bundle sheath
Statement III: Phloem parenchyma is absent in the vascular bundles of monocot stems
Statement IV: Water-containing cavities are also absent within the vascular bundles of monocot stems
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and II are correct
Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Water-containing cavities are present within the vascular bundles of monocot stems

Question:
Assertion (A): Bulliform cells in grasses help minimize water loss during water stress
Reason (R): When flaccid, bulliform cells make the leaves curl inwards, thereby exposing less surface area to the atmosphere
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both (A) and (R) are true. The inward curling (R) caused by flaccidity is the mechanism that achieves the minimization of water loss (A)

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: In an isobilateral leaf, stomata are present on both surfaces of the epidermis
Statement II: Mesophyll in monocot leaves is differentiated into palisade and spongy parenchyma
Statement III: Parallel venation in monocot leaves results in near similar sizes of vascular bundles
Statement IV: Vascular bundles in leaves are surrounded by thick-walled bundle sheath cells
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: In isobilateral leaves, the mesophyll is not differentiated.

Question:
Match the characteristic in Column I with the stem type in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Ring bundles(i)Monocotyledonous stem
B.Scattered bundles(ii)Location in a stem bundle
C.Water cavities(iii)Dicotyledonous stem
D.Phloem on outer side(iv)Present in monocot bundles
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iii, B-i, C-iv, D-ii

Question:
Statement I: The trichomes in the shoot system are usually unicellular, branched or unbranched and soft or stiff
Statement II: In a dorsiventral leaf, the palisade parenchyma is placed adaxially
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: The trichomes in the shoot system are usually multicellular, may be branched or unbranched and soft or stiff.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: The epidermal tissue system comprises epidermal cells, stomata, and epidermal appendages like trichomes and hairs
Statement II: Epidermal cells are parenchymatous with a small amount of cytoplasm lining the cell wall and a large vacuole
Statement III: The cuticle is a waxy thick layer that prevents water loss and is present in both stems and roots
Statement IV: Stomata are structures present in the epidermis of leaves that regulate transpiration and gaseous exchange
Options: Statements I, II and IV are correct
Statements I and II are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: The cuticle is explicitly stated to be absent in roots.

Question:
Assertion (A): All tissues on the inner side of the endodermis, such as pericycle and vascular bundles, constitute the stele
Reason (R): The endodermis is the innermost layer of the cortex and contains water-impermeable casparian strips
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true. However, the composition of the stele (A) is not explained by the chemical nature of the endodermal wall (R)

Question:
Match the appendage in Column I with its characteristic in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Root hairs(i)Usually multicellular shoot hairs
B.Trichomes(ii)Unicellular elongations for absorption
C.Cuticle(iii)Stomatal regulatory cells
D.Guard cells(iv)Waxy layer absent in roots
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question:
Statement I: The anatomy of the monocot root is similar to the dicot root in having a small or inconspicuous pith
Statement II: Monocot roots usually have more than six (polyarch) xylem bundles
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: The pith in monocot roots is large and well developed, whereas it is small/inconspicuous in dicot roots. Statement II is correct.

Question:
Statement I: The bulliform cells in grasses are large, empty and pigmented cells
Statement II: When bulliform cells are turgid, they make the leaves curl inwards to minimize water loss
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both statements are incorrect. Bulliform cells are colourless (not pigmented). They cause leaves to curl when they are flaccid due to water stress, not when they are turgid

Question:
Assertion (A): Dicotyledonous stems are described as having "open" vascular bundles
Reason (R): In dicotyledonous stems, cambium is present between the phloem and xylem, allowing for the formation of secondary tissues
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A). The term "open" refers specifically to the presence of cambium and the resulting ability for secondary growth

Question:
Statement I: Echinoderms are characterized by the presence of a water vascular system
Statement II: The water vascular system in echinoderms helps in locomotion and capture of food but not in respiration
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: The water vascular system does help in respiration.

Question:
Statement I: ICBN stands for International Code for Botanical Nomenclature and deals with the naming of plants
Statement II: ICZN stands for International Code of Zoological Nomenclature and provides rules for naming animals
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Family, has a group of related genera with higher number of similarities as compared to genus and species
Statement II: Taxon is a unit of classification representing a rank
Statement III: Kingdom is the lowest category in the taxonomic hierarchy
Statement IV: All taxonomic categories together constitute the taxonomic hierarchy
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Family, has a group of related genera with still less number of similarities as compared to genus and species
Statement III is incorrect: Species is the lowest category; Kingdom is the highest

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: Taxonomy is the study of principles and procedures of classification
Statement II: Categorization is the process by which anything is grouped into convenient categories based on some easily observable characters
Statement III: Nomenclature or naming is only possible when the organism is described correctly and we know to what organism the name is attached to
Statement IV: As we explore new areas, and even old ones, new organisms are continuously being identified
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Statements III and IV are correct.
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Classification is the process by which anything is grouped into convenient categories based on some easily observable characters

Question:
Match the organism in Column I with its Genus or Family in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Tiger(i)Genus Solanum
B.Potato(ii)Family Felidae
C.Cat(iii)Family Solanaceae
D.Petunia(iv)Genus Panthera
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iv, B-i, C-ii, D-iii

Question:
Assertion (A): Osteichthyes must swim constantly to avoid sinking
Reason (R): Bony fishes lack an air bladder which regulates buoyancy
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
Both A and R are false
Solution:
Assertion is false: Chondrichthyes (cartilaginous fish) must swim constantly to avoid sinking.
Reason is false: Bony fishes do possess an air bladder for buoyancy

Question:
Assertion (A): Platyhelminthes are generally called flatworms
Reason (R): They have a dorso-ventrally flattened body
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The name "flatworm" (A) is a direct reference to their specific dorso-ventrally flattened body shape (R)

Question:
Statement I: Molluscs are usually monoecious and oviparous with direct development
Statement II: The mouth of molluscs contains a file like rasping organ for feeding called the radula
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Molluscs are usually dioecious and oviparous with indirect development.

Question: Match Column I with Column II:
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i

Question:
Match the study aspect in Column I with its requirement in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Modern Taxonomy(i)Ecological information
B.Systematics(ii)Observable characters
C.Basic Taxonomy(iii)Evolutionary history
D.Classification(iv)Identification & Nomenclature
Solution: The correct matching is: A-i, B-iii, C-iv, D-ii

Question:
Statement I: Chondrichthyes have a streamlined body and a cartilaginous endoskeleton
Statement II: In cartilaginous fishes, the gill slits are separate and covered by an operculum
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Cartilaginous fish lack an operculum

Question:
Statement I: Characterisation, identification, nomenclature and classification are the processes that are basic to taxonomy
Statement II: In binomial nomenclature, the first word denotes the specific epithet and the second word denotes the genus
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: The first word is the Genus and the second word is the specific epithet.

Question: Match the animal in Column I with the type of Coelom in Column II:
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: Genus comprises a group of related species
Statement II: Potato and brinjal are two different species but both belong to the genus Solanum
Statement III: Lion, leopard, and tiger are species of the genus Panthera
Statement IV: Genus Panthera includes the common house cat
Options: Statements II and IV are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: The house cat belongs to the genus Felis

Question:
Statement I: A genus is a group of related species which has more characters in common in comparison to species of other genera
Statement II: Panthera leo (lion) and Felis catus (cat) both belong to the same genus Panthera
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Lion belongs to the genus Panthera, while the house cat belongs to the genus Felis.

Question:
Assertion (A): Ctenophores are commonly known as "sea walnuts" or "comb jellies"
Reason (R): The body of ctenophores bears eight external rows of ciliated comb plates
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The presence of eight ciliated comb plates (R) is the morphological reason they are referred to as comb jellies (A)

Question:
Statement I: Platyhelminthes are mostly endoparasites found in animals including human beings
Statement II: Specialized cells called flame cells in flatworms help in respiration and circulation
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Flame cells help in osmoregulation and excretion, not respiration or circulation.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: Echinoderms have an endoskeleton of calcareous ossicles
Statement II: The most distinctive feature of echinoderms is the presence of a water vascular system
Statement III: The water vascular system helps in locomotion, capture and transport of food, and respiration
Statement IV: An excretory system is well-developed in echinoderms
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: An excretory system is absent in echinoderms.

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: Chordates are characterized by the presence of a notochord and a dorsal hollow nerve cord
Statement II: In Urochordata, notochord is present only in larval tail
Statement III: In Cephalochordata, notochord extends from head to tail region and is not persistent throughout their life
Statement IV: All vertebrates are chordates but all chordates are not vertebrates
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I, II and IV are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: In Cephalochordata, notochord extends from head to tail region and is persistent throughout their life

Question:
Assertion (A): All organisms belonging to various phyla are assigned to the highest category called Kingdom Animalia
Reason (R): Kingdom is the most specific and lowest category in the taxonomic hierarchy
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is false: Kingdom is the highest and most generalized category; Species is the lowest and most specific.

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: Binomial nomenclature was developed by Carolus Linnaeus
Statement II: Biological names are generally in Latin and written in italics
Statement III: The first word in a biological name represents the specific epithet
Statement IV: Both words in a biological name, when handwritten, are underlined together
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements I and II are correct
Solution:
Statement III is incorrect: The first word represents the Genus, while the second component denotes the specific epithet
Statement IV is incorrect: Both words in a biological name, when handwritten, are separately underlined

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: Ctenophores, commonly known as sea walnuts or comb jellies, are exclusively marine
Statement II: The body bears eight external rows of ciliated comb plates, which help in locomotion
Statement III: Digestion in Ctenophora is only intracellular
Statement IV: Bioluminescence is well-marked in ctenophores
Options: Statements I, II and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: Digestion in Ctenophora is both extracellular and intracellular.

Question: Match Column I with Column II:
Solution: The correct matching is: A-iv, B-iii, C-i, D-ii

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: Class Dicotyledonae and Monocotyledonae are included in the division Angiospermae
Statement II: Order Primata and Carnivora are included in class Mammalia
Statement III: Fishes, amphibians, reptiles, and birds constitute the next higher category called Phylum Chordata
Statement IV: Phylum is the term used for plants, while Division is used for animals
Options: Statements I and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Phylum is used for animals, while Division is used for plants.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: In sponges, water enters through minute pores called ostia in the body wall into a central cavity, spongocoel
Statement II: Choanocytes or collar cells line the coelenteron and the canal
Statement III: Digestion in Porifera is extracellular
Statement IV: Sponges have a water transport or canal system which helps in food gathering and respiratory exchange
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Choanocytes or collar cells line the spongocoel and the canal
Statement III is incorrect: Digestion in sponges is intracellular

Question:
Statement I: Reproduction in comb jellies takes place only by asexual means
Statement II: Ctenophores, commonly known as sea walnuts, are exclusively marine and exhibit bioluminescence
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Reproduction in comb jellies takes place only by sexual means

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Annelids are aquatic or terrestrial, free living, and sometimes parasitic
Statement II: They possess longitudinal and circular muscles which help in locomotion
Statement III: Nephridia help in osmoregulation and excretion
Statement IV: Neural system consists of paired ganglia connected by lateral nerves to a double ventral nerve cord
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Solution: All statements are correct.

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Platyhelminthes have a dorso-ventrally flattened body, hence are called flatworms
Statement II: They are mostly endoparasites found in animals including human beings
Statement III: Specialized cells called nephridial cells help in osmoregulation and excretion
Statement IV: Fertilization is external and development is through many larval stages
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement III is incorrect: Specialized cells called flame cells help in osmoregulation and excretion

Question: Match the Phylum in Column I with its characteristic feature in Column II:
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question:
Match the Taxonomic Group in Column I with its members in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Genus Panthera(i)Monkey, Gorilla, Gibbon
B.Genus Felis(ii)Lion, Tiger, Leopard
C.Order Primata(iii)Potato, Brinjal
D.Genus Solanum(iv)Common House Cat
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii

Question:
Assertion (A): The circulatory system in Mollusca is of the closed type
Reason (R): Molluscs have a soft and spongy layer of skin forming a mantle over the visceral hump
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is false: Molluscs (except cephalopods like octopus) generally have an open circulatory system.

Question:
Read the following statements about frog carefully and select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: Chondrichthyes are marine animals with a streamlined body and a cartilaginous endoskeleton
Statement II: The skin is tough, containing minute placoid scales
Statement III: In Chondrichthyes, the gill slits are separate and covered by an operculum
Statement IV: They must swim constantly to avoid sinking as they lack an air bladder
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: In Chondrichthyes (cartilaginous fishes), gill slits are without an operculum (gill cover).

Question:
Assertion (A): Roundworms are characterized as pseudocoelomates
Reason (R): In Aschelminthes, the body cavity is not lined by mesoderm; instead, the mesoderm is present as scattered pouches
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The definition of a pseudocoelom (A) is specifically the presence of mesoderm in scattered pouches between the ectoderm and endoderm (R).

Question:
Statement I: Nereis, an aquatic form of annelid, is dioecious, but earthworms and leeches are monoecious
Statement II: A closed circulatory system is present in all annelids except Nereis
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Nereis, an aquatic form of annelid, is dioecious, but earthworms and leeches are monoecious.
Statement II is incorrect: Annelids, including Nereis generally have a closed circulatory system

Question:
Match the acronym in Column I with its full form in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A. ICBN(i) International Code of Zoological Nomenclature
B. ICZN(ii) International Code for Botanical Nomenclature
C. Biodiversity(iii) Evolutionary relationships
D. Systematics(iv) Number and types of organisms
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii

Question:
Assertion (A): Higher the category, greater is the difficulty of determining the relationship to other taxa at the same level
Reason (R): In the taxonomic hierarchy, Genus comprises a group of related species which has more characters in common in comparison to species of other genera
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are correct. However, the definition/description of a Genus (R) does not explain the logistical and biological complexity of determining relationships at higher levels like Phylum or Kingdom (A).

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: Man belongs to the family Hominidae and order Primata
Statement II: Housefly belongs to the family Muscidae and order Diptera
Statement III: Mango belongs to the family Anacardiaceae and order Sapindales
Statement IV: Wheat belongs to the family Poaceae and order Poales
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Solution: All statements are correct

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: Hemichordates are a small group of worm-like marine animals with organ-system level of organization
Statement II: In Saccoglossus, sexes are not separate
Statement III: Circulatory system is of closed type
Statement IV: Respiration takes place through gills and excretion is by proboscis gland
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: In Saccoglossus, sexes are separate
Statement III is incorrect: The circulatory system in Hemichordata is of the open type.

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: Species is a group of individual organisms with fundamental similarities
Statement II: Panthera leo and Panthera tigris belong to the same genus
Statement III: Solanum nigrum and Solanum melongena belong to the same specific epithet
Statement IV: One should be able to distinguish one species from other closely related species based on morphological differences
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: nigrum and melongena are different specific epithets; they belong to the same genus (Solanum)

Question:

Question:
Assertion (A): The Bohr model of the atom cannot be applied to the helium atom.
Reason (R): The Bohr model does not account for electron-electron interactions.
Options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
Solution: The Bohr model is only applicable to single-electron species (like $\mathrm{H}, \mathrm{He}^{+}, \mathrm{Li}^{++}$) because it only considers the electrostatic attraction between one nucleus and one electron, ignoring the repulsive forces between multiple electrons. The helium atom has two electrons.

Question: Time taken for an electron to complete one revolution in Bohr orbit of hydrogen atom is
Solution:
Distance to complete one revolution in the Bohr’s orbit, d = \[2\pi r\]
Also, angular momentum of an electron by Bohr is:
                          mvr = $\frac{{nh}}{{2\pi }} $
$\Rightarrow$                         v = $\frac{{nh}}{{2\pi mr}}$
We know,              
                         Velocity = $\frac{{\text{Distance}}}{{\text{Time}}}$      
                   $\therefore$   Time = $\frac{{d}}{{v}}$ =  $\frac{{2\pi r}}{{nh/2\pi mr}} = \frac{{4{\pi ^2}m{r^2}}}{{nh}}$

Tags.Subject: Physics
Tags.Topic: Bohr Model of the Hydrogen Atom

Question:
Assertion (A): In Simple Harmonic Motion, the velocity of the particle is maximum at the mean position.
Reason (R): The acceleration of a particle performing SHM is directly proportional to its displacement from the mean position and is always directed towards the mean position.
Options: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.
Solution: At the mean position ( $x = 0$ ), velocity $v = \omega \sqrt {{A^2} - {x^2}} $ becomes maximum ( $v = A\omega $ ). The Reason is a correct statement of the definition of SHM ( $a = - {\omega ^2}x$ ), but it doesn't directly explain why velocity is maximum at the center (which is due to the conversion of potential energy to kinetic energy).

Question: A uniform magnetic field B exists in a region. An electron projected perpendicular to the field goes in a circle. Assuming Bohr’s quantization rule for angular momentum is valid, then the smallest possible radius of orbit of electron is:
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  r = \frac{{mv}}{{qB}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,mvr = \frac{{nh}}{{2\pi }} \\
  \therefore \,\,\,{r^2} = \frac{h}{{2\pi qB}}\,\,\,\left( {{\text{least}}} \right) \\
  \therefore \,\,r = \sqrt {\frac{h}{{2\pi eB}}} \\
\end{gathered} \]

Question: A ground-state electron (in a hydrogen atom) absorb a photon of energy 3E<sub>0</sub>. How much kinetic energy does the electron now have? (\[{{\text{E}}_0}\]is the energy in the ground state of electron)
Solution:
Energy of electron in ground state = \[ - {{\text{E}}_0}\]
So total final energy = \[ - {{\text{E}}_0} + 3{{\text{E}}_0} = 2{{\text{E}}_0}\]

Question:
Assertion: The kinetic energy of the electron in an orbit of radius r in hydrogen atom is (e = electronic charge) $\frac{{{e^2}}}{{2r}}$ in CGS unit.
Reason: For orbiting electron KE = $\frac{1}{2}$ |PE|.
Options: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and the Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but the Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is true, but Reason is false.
Both Assertion and Reason are false.
Solution:
Potential energy of electron in nth orbit of radius r in Hydrogen atom is$U = - \frac{{{e^2}}}{r}\left( {in\,\,CGS} \right)$
Since, KE =$\frac{1}{2}$ $\left| {\,PE\,} \right|$, therefore, $K = \frac{{{e^2}}}{{2r}}.$

Tags.Subject: Physics
Tags.Topic: Bohr Model of the Hydrogen Atom

Question: The radius of the hydrogen atom in its ground state is  $ {a_0} $ . The radius of a muonic hydrogen atom, in which the electron is replaced by an identically charged muon with mass  $ 207 $  times that of electron,  $ {a_\mu } $  is equal to:
Solution:
We know that $ {a_0} = \frac{{{ \in _0}{h^2}}}{{\pi m{e^2}}} $ …. (i)
Hence, $ {a_\mu } = \frac{{{h^2}{ \in _0}}}{{\pi \left( {207m} \right){e^2}}} $ …. (ii)
Dividing eqn. (ii) by eqn. (i), we get:
 $ \frac{{{a_\mu }}}{{{a_0}}} = \frac{1}{{207}} $ or $ {a_\mu } = \frac{{{a_0}}}{{207}} $

Tags.Subject: Physics

Question: The de Broglie wavelength of an electron in 2<sup>nd</sup> orbit of hydrogen atom is: ( $ {a_0} =  $ Bohr’s radius)
Solution:
radius of $ {n^{th}} $ orbit is
 $ {r_n} = {n^2}{a_0} $ , where $ {a_0} $ is the Bohr’s radius. If $ \lambda $ is the de Broglie wavelength of an electron while revolving in $ {n^{th}} $ orbit of radius $ {r_n} $ , then
 $ 2\pi {r_n} = n\lambda $
or $ \lambda = \frac{{2\pi {r_n}}}{n} $
For 2nd orbit, $ n = 2 $
 $ \therefore \,\,\lambda = \frac{{2\pi {r_2}}}{2} = \frac{{2\pi {{\left( 2 \right)}^2}{a_0}}}{2} = 4\pi {a_0} $

Question:
An ideal gas undergoes a cyclic process as shown in Figure.

\[\Delta {U_{BC}}\; = {\rm{ }} - {\rm{ }}5{\rm{ }}kJ{\rm{ }}mo{l^{ - \;}}^1,{\rm{ }}{{\rm{Q}}_{AB}}_\; = {\rm{ }}2{\rm{ }}kJ{\rm{ }}mo{l^{ - \;}}^1\]
\[{W_{AB}}\; = \;{\rm{ }}5{\rm{ }}kJ{\rm{ }}mo{l^{ - \;}}^1,{\rm{ }}{W_{CA}}\; = {\rm{ }} - 3{\rm{ }}kJ{\rm{ }}mo{l^{ - \;}}^1\]
Heat absorbed by the system during process CA is:
Solution:
$$AB \to {\text{isobaric}}$$

$$BC \to {\text{isochoric}}$$

$$CA \to {\text{not}}\,{\text{defined}}$$

For AB

\[Q = \Delta U + W\]                 (First law of thermodynamics)

\[\Delta U_{AB} = 2 - 5 =  - 3\,kJ/mol\]

For ABC

$$\Delta {U_{ABC}} = \Delta {U_{AB}} + \Delta {U_{BC}}$$

\[ = {\rm{ }} - 3{\rm{ }} - {\rm{ }}5{\rm{ }} = {\rm{ }} - 8{\rm{ }}kJ/mol\]

For cyclic process $\Delta U = 0$

\[\Delta {U_{ABC}}+\Delta {U_{CA}} =0\\\Rightarrow \Delta U_{CA}= + 8\,kJ/mol\]

\[\Delta U_{CA} = {\rm{ }}Q_{CA}{\rm{  -  }}W_{CA}\]

\[8{\rm{ }} = {\rm{ }}Q_{CA}{\rm{ }} + {\rm{ }}3\]

\[Q_{CA}{\rm{ }} = {\rm{ }} + 5{\rm{ kJ/mol}}\]

Tags.Topic: Alpha-Particle Scattering and Rutherford's Nuclear Model of Atom

Question:
An electron of a stationary hydrogen atom passes from the fifth energy level to the ground level. The velocity that the atom acquired as a result of photon emission will be
(m is the mass of the electron, R is Rydberg constant and h is Planck’s constant)
Solution:
Here, ${E_5} - {E_1} = \frac{{hc}}{\lambda }$
and $\frac{{Rhc}}{{25}} - Rhc = \frac{{hc}}{\lambda } \Rightarrow \frac{{24}}{{25}}R = \frac{1}{\lambda }$
But $p = \frac{h}{\lambda }$and $v = \frac{h}{{m\lambda }} = \frac{{24}}{{25}}\frac{{Rh}}{m}$

Question: In a hydrogen atom, when an electron moves from the orbit n = 1 to n = 4, then wavelength of the radiations absorbed will be (R is Rydberg’s constant):
Solution:
Wavelength is given by

$$\frac{1}{\lambda } = R\left( {\frac{1}{{{n_1}^2}} - \frac{1}{{{n_2}^2}}} \right) \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\lambda } = R\left[ {\frac{1}{{{{\left( 1 \right)}^2}}} - \frac{1}{{{{\left( 4 \right)}^2}}}} \right]$$

$$ \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\lambda } = R\left( {\frac{1}{1} - \frac{1}{{16}}} \right) \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\lambda } = R\left( {\frac{{16 - 1}}{{16}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\lambda } = \frac{{15R}}{{16}} \Rightarrow \lambda  = \frac{{16}}{{15R}}$$

Question: A hydrogen sample is in 4th excited state. The ratio of shortest wavelength of Lyman and shortest wavelength of Balmer series in radiation emitted by this sample is
Solution:
"THIS IS A HIGH WEIGHTAGE TOPIC"


$\frac{1}{\lambda } = R{z^2}\left( {\frac{1}{{n_1^2}} - \frac{1}{{n_2^2}}} \right)$

$\frac{1}{{{\lambda _1}}} = R{z^2}\left( {\frac{1}{{{1^2}}} - \frac{1}{{{5^2}}}} \right) = R{z^2}\left( {1 - \frac{1}{{25}}} \right) = R{z^2} \times \frac{{24}}{{25}}$

$\frac{1}{{{\lambda_2}}} = R{z^2}\left( {\frac{1}{{{2^2}}} - \frac{1}{{{5^2}}}} \right) = R{z^2}\left( {\frac{1}{4} - \frac{1}{{25}}} \right) = R{z^2}\left( {\frac{{21}}{{25 \times 4}}} \right)$

$ \Rightarrow \frac{{{\lambda _1}}}{{{\lambda _2}}} = \left( {\frac{1}{{24}}} \right)\left( {\frac{{21}}{4}} \right) = \left( {\frac{7}{{32}}} \right)$

Question:
Assertion (A): If the frequency of oscillation of a particle in SHM is $f$, the frequency of its kinetic energy oscillations is $2f$.
Reason (R): Kinetic energy becomes maximum twice in one complete cycle of SHM.
Options: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.
Solution: K.E. $ = \frac{1}{2}m{\omega ^2}{A^2}{\text{co}}{{\text{s}}^2}\left( {\omega t} \right)$. Using ${\text{co}}{{\text{s}}^2}\theta = \left( {1 + {\text{cos}}2\theta } \right)/2$, we see the angular frequency of energy is $2\omega $. Thus, frequency is $2f$. The reason explains why this happens visually (maximum speed at mean position happens twice per cycle).

Question:
Assertion (A): When a spring of spring constant $k$ is cut into two equal halves, the spring constant of each half becomes $2k$.
Reason (R): The spring constant $k$ is inversely proportional to the natural length $l$ of the spring ($k \propto 1/l$).
Options: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.
Solution: For a spring, the product $k \times l$ is constant. If length is halved, $k$ must double. This increases the frequency of a spring-block system if the same mass is used.

Question:
Assertion (A): If the length of a simple pendulum is increased four times, its time period is doubled.
Reason (R): The time period of a simple pendulum is directly proportional to the square root of its length.
Options: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.
Solution: $T \propto \sqrt L $. If $L' = 4L$, then $T' = \sqrt 4 T = 2T$. The reason correctly identifies the square root relationship.

Question:
Assertion (A): At the extreme positions of SHM, the potential energy of the particle is maximum.
Reason (R): At extreme positions, the velocity of the particle is zero.
Options: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.
Solution: Both are true. At $x = A,U = \frac{1}{2}k{A^2}\left( {{\text{Max}}} \right)$. At $x = A$, $v = 0$. However, $v = 0$ explains why Kinetic Energy is minimum, it doesn't "cause" the Potential Energy to be maximum (that is caused by the displacement $x$ being maximum).

Question:
Assertion (A): The time period of a simple pendulum increases when it is taken inside a lift accelerating upwards.
Reason (R): The effective acceleration due to gravity ( ${g_{eff}}$ ) decreases when a lift accelerates upwards.
Options: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.
Solution:
When a lift accelerates up with $'a'$, ${g_{eff}} = g + a$. Since $T = 2\pi \sqrt {L/{g_{{\text{eff\;}}}}} $, an increase in
${g_{eff}}$ results in a decrease in the time period. Both A and R are false, but in standard NEET options, if A is false and R is false, it's often D or E. Here, R is false as stated.

Question:
Assertion (A): The total mechanical energy of a particle performing SHM is constant, provided there are no dissipative forces.
Reason (R): In SHM, there is a continuous exchange between kinetic energy and potential energy, and their sum $E = \frac{1}{2}m{\omega ^2}{A^2}$ remains invariant.
Options: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion (A) is true but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false but Reason (R) is true.
Solution: The total energy is the sum of K.E. and P.E. Since $K = \frac{1}{2}m{\omega ^2}\left( {{A^2} - {x^2}} \right)$ and $U = \frac{1}{2}m{\omega ^2}{x^2}$, adding them gives $E = \frac{1}{2}m{\omega ^2}{A^2}$. The reason correctly explains the conservation.

Question: Solve for x: \[x + \frac{5}{{2x}} = \frac{7}{2}\]
Solution:
We have: \[x + \frac{5}{{2x}} = \frac{7}{2}\]
Multiplying throughout by 2x:
\[2{x^2} + 5 = 7x\,\, \Rightarrow \,\,2{x^2}-7x + 5 = 0\]
Comparing with ax2+bx +c =0; \[a \ne 0,\]
a = 2, b = –7, = 5

\[x = \frac{{ - b \pm \sqrt {{b^2} - 4ac} }}{{2a}} = \frac{{ - \left( { - 7} \right) \pm \sqrt {{{\left( { - 7} \right)}^2} - 4 \times 2 \times 5} }}{{2 \times 2}}\]

\[ = \frac{{7 \pm 3}}{4}\]
\[ = \frac{5}{2}or1\]

Question: If equations \[2{x^2} + kx - 5 = 0\,\,\,and\,{x^2} - 3x - 4 = 0\] a common root, then the value of k are.
Solution:
Let a be a root common to both of the given equation 

$$\therefore 2{a^2} + ka - 5 = 0$$

and $${a^2} - 3a - 4 = 0$$

On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$\frac{{{a^2}}}{{\left( { - 4k - 15} \right)}} = \frac{a}{{ - 5 + 8}} = \frac{1}{{ - 6 - k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,{a^2} = \frac{{4k + 15}}{{k + 6}}\,\,{\text{and}}\,a = \frac{{ - 3}}{{k + 6}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left( {\frac{{ - 3}}{{k + 6}}} \right) = \frac{{4k + 15}}{{k + 6}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left( {4k + 15} \right)\left( {k + 6} \right) = 9$$

$$ \Rightarrow 4{k^2} + 39k + 81 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow k =  - 3\,\,{\text{or}}\,\,k =  - \frac{{27}}{4}$$

Question: Find the area under the curve $y = {x^2} + 1$ from $x = 1$ to $x = 3$
Solution:
Area under the curve $I = \int\limits_{x - 1}^{x - 3} {\left( {{x^2} + 1} \right)dx} $
$ = \,\left[ {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + x} \right]_1^3 = \left[ {\frac{{{3^3}}}{3} + 3} \right] - \left[ {\frac{{{1^3}}}{3} + 1} \right]$
$ = 12 - \frac{1}{3} - 1 = \frac{{32}}{3}$

Question: A sample of ideal gas $\left( {\gamma = 1.4} \right)$ is heated at constant pressure. If an amount of 100 J heat is supplied to the gas, the work done by the gas is
Solution:
$Q = n{C_p}\,\Delta T$
$100 = n\frac{7}{2}R\Delta T$
$n\Delta T = \frac{{200}}{{7R}}$
$Q = \Delta U + W$
$100 = n{C_v}\Delta T + W$
$100 = \frac{{200}}{{7R}} \times \frac{{5R}}{2} + W$
$W = 28.57\,J$

Question:
A car travels 6 km towards north at an angle of\[45^\circ \] to the east and then travels distance of 4 km towards north at an angle\[135^\circ \] of to the east. How far is the point from the starting point? What angle does the straight line joining its initial and final position makes with the east?
(a) \[\sqrt {50} \,km\,and{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( 5 \right)\]
(b) \[10\,km\,and{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\sqrt 5 } \right)\]
(c) \[\sqrt {52} \,\,km\,\,and\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( 5 \right)\]
(d) \[\sqrt {52} \,\,km\,\,and\,{\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {\sqrt 5 } \right)\]
Solution:
Net movement along x- direction
\[{S_x} = \left( {6 - 4} \right)\cos 45^\circ \hat i = 2 \times \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} = \sqrt 2 km\]
Net movement along y- direction
\[{S_y} = \left( {6 + 4} \right)\sin 45^\circ \hat j = 10 \times \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} = 5\sqrt 2 km\]
Net movement from starting point
\[\left| {\overrightarrow S } \right| = \sqrt {{S_x}^2 + {S_y}^2} = \sqrt {{{\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2} + {{\left( {5\sqrt 2 } \right)}^2}} = \sqrt {52} km\]
Angle which makes with the east direction
\[\tan \theta = \frac{{Y - component}}{{X - component}} = \frac{{5\sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 2 }}\therefore \theta = {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( 5 \right)\]

Question: If $y = \left[ {2{x^3} + 3} \right]\left[ {2{x^{ - 3}} + 1} \right],$then find $\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}$.
Solution:
Here $u = 2{x^3} + 3,v = 2{x^{ - 3}} + 1$
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get
$\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{d}{{dx}}\left[ {\left( {2{x^3} + 3} \right)\left( {2{x^{ - 3}} + 1} \right)} \right]$
Using product rule, $\frac{{d\left( {uv} \right)}}{{dx}} = u\frac{{dv}}{{dx}} + v\frac{{du}}{{dx}}$
$\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \left[ {2{x^3} + 3} \right]\frac{d}{{dx}}\left[ {2{x^{ - 3}} + 1} \right] + \left[ {2{x^{ - 3}} + 1} \right]\frac{d}{{dx}}\left[ {2{x^3} + 3} \right]$
$ = \left[ {2{x^3} + 3} \right]\left[ { - 6{x^{ - 4}}} \right] + \left[ {2{x^{ - 3}} + 1} \right]\left[ {6{x^2}} \right]$
$ = \left[ {2{x^3} + 3} \right]\left[ {\frac{{ - 6}}{{{x^4}}}} \right] + \left[ {\frac{2}{{{x^3}}} + 1} \right]\left[ {6{x^2}} \right] = - \frac{{18}}{{{x^4}}} + 6{x^2}$

Question: If $\vec A = 3\hat i + 4\hat j$and $\vec B = 7\hat i + 24\hat j$, the vector having the same magnitude as \[\vec B\] and parallel to \[\vec A\] is
Solution:
$\vec r = \left| B \right|\hat A$

$$\left| {\vec A} \right| = \sqrt {{3^2} + {4^2}}  = 5$$

also $$\left| {\vec B} \right| = \sqrt {{7^2} + {{24}^2}}  = 25$$

$${\text{desired}}\,{\text{vector}} = \left| {\vec B} \right|\hat A = 25 \times \frac{{3\hat i + 4\hat j}}{5}$$

$$ = 5\left( {3\hat i + 4\hat j} \right) = 15\hat i + 20\hat j.$$

Question: If the sum of the two unit vectors is also a unit vector, then magnitude of their difference is
Solution:
At 120°
The resultant of 2 equal forces are equal to a force of same magnitude.
$\begin{gathered}
  \left| {\vec A} \right| = 1 \\
  \left| {\vec B} \right| = 1 \\
  \left| {\vec A + \vec B} \right| = 1\;\;({\text{given}}) \\
\end{gathered} $

Hence,
$\vec A - \vec B$

$\left| {\vec A - \vec B} \right| = \sqrt {{A^2} + {B^2} + 2AB\cos 60^\circ } $
$\sqrt {1 + 1 + 2\cos 60^\circ } = \sqrt 3 $

Question: $y = a\cos \theta ,\,\,\,x = a\sin \theta ;\,\,\,{\text{a}} = {\text{ constant}}$. Find dy/dx.
Solution:
$$\frac{y}{a} = \cos \theta $$

$$\frac{x}{a} = \sin \theta $$

Eliminate $$\theta $$ 

Square + add

$${\sin ^2}\theta  + {\cos ^2}\theta  = 1$$

$$\frac{{{y^2}}}{{{a^2}}} + \frac{{{x^2}}}{{{a^2}}} = 1$$

$$\therefore {y^2} = {a^2} - {x^2}$$

$$y = \sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} $$

$$\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\left( { - 2x} \right)}}{{2\sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} }} = \frac{{ - x}}{{\sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} }}$$

Question: If a vector \[2\hat i + 3\hat j + 8\hat k\] is perpendicular to the vector \[4\hat j -4\hat i + \alpha \hat k.\] Then the value of $\alpha$  is
Solution:
Given vectors can be rewritten as \[\vec A = 2\hat i + 3\hat j + 8\hat k\,\,{\text{and}}\,\,\vec B = - 4\hat i + 4\hat j + \alpha \hat k\]
Dot product of these vectors should be equal to zero because they are perpendicular.
\[\therefore \vec A \cdot \vec B = - 8 + 12 + 8\alpha = 0\,\, \Rightarrow 8\alpha = - 4\,\,\, \Rightarrow \alpha = - 1/2\]

Question: The angle between the two vectors \[\overrightarrow A  = 3\hat i + 4\hat j + 5\hat k\,\,\,\,\,\,and\,\,\,\,\overrightarrow B  = 3\hat i + 4\hat j + 5\hat k\] is
Solution:
\[\begin{gathered}
  \cos \theta = \frac{{\overrightarrow {A.} \overrightarrow B }}{{\left| A \right|\left| B \right|}} = \frac{{9 + 16 + 25}}{{\sqrt {9 + 16 + 25} \sqrt {9 + 16 + 25} }} \\
   = \frac{{50}}{{50}} = 1 \Rightarrow \cos \theta = 1\therefore \theta = {\cos ^{ - 1}}\left( 1 \right) \\
\end{gathered} \]
 = 00

Question: Find the value of $\tan \frac{{7\pi }}{{12}}$
Solution:
$$\tan \left( {\frac{{7\pi }}{{12}}} \right) = \tan \left( {\pi  - \frac{{5\pi }}{{12}}} \right)$$

$$ =  - \tan \left( {\frac{{5\pi }}{{12}}} \right)$$

$$ =  - \tan \left( {\frac{{3\pi }}{{12}} + \frac{{2\pi }}{{12}}} \right)$$

$$ =  - \tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} + \frac{\pi }{6}} \right)$$

Now using $$\tan \left( {A + B} \right) = \frac{{\tan A + \tan B}}{{1 - \tan A\tan B}}$$

$$ =  - \frac{{\tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right) + \tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{6}} \right)}}{{1 - \tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4}} \right)\tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{6}} \right)}}$$

$$ =  - \frac{{1 + \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}}}{{1 - 1 \times \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}}} =  - \frac{{\sqrt 3  + 1}}{{\sqrt 3  - 1}}$$

Question:
Read the assertion and reason carefully to mark the correct option out of the options given below:
Assertion: The scalar product of two vectors can be zero.
Reason: If two vectors are perpendicular to each other, their scalar product will be zero.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and the reason is the correct explanation of the assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of the assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If the assertion and reason both are false.
Solution:
If θ be the angle between two vectors $\vec A$ and $\vec B$, then their scalar product, $\vec A.\vec B = AB\cos \theta $
If $\theta = 90^\circ $ then $\vec A.\vec B = 0$

i.e. if $\vec A$ and $\vec B$ are perpendicular to each other then their scalar product will be zero.

Question: Let \[\vec A\, = \,\left( {\hat i\, + \,\hat j} \right)\,\,and\,\,\vec B\, = \,\left( {2\hat i\, - \,\hat j} \right).\,\,\]The magnitude of a coplanar vector \[\vec C\] such that  \[\vec A\,.\,\vec C\, = \,\vec B\,.\,\vec C\, = \,\vec A\,.\,\vec B,\,\,is\,\,given\,\,by\,\,:\]
Solution:
let the vector
\[\vec C\] be \[x\hat i{\mathbf{ + }}y\hat j\]
\[\vec A.\vec C{\mathbf{ = }}x{\mathbf{ + }}y{\mathbf{ = }}1\]
and \[\vec B.\vec C{\mathbf{ = }}2x{\mathbf{ - }}y{\mathbf{ = }}1\]
Solving these two equations simultaneously
\[x{\mathbf{ = }}\frac{2}{3}\] and \[y{\mathbf{ = }}\frac{1}{3}\]
Hence \[\left| {\vec C} \right|{\mathbf{ = }}\sqrt {\frac{4}{9}{\mathbf{ + }}\frac{1}{9}} {\mathbf{ = }}\sqrt {\frac{5}{9}} \]

Question:
Read the assertion and reason carefully to mark the correct option out of the options given below:
Assertion: The sum of two vectors can be zero.
Reason: The vector cancel each other, when they are equal and opposite.
Options: If both assertion and reason are true and the reason is the correct explanation of the assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of the assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If the assertion and reason both are false.
Solution:
Let $\vec P$ and $\vec Q$ are two vectors in opposite direction, then their sum $\vec P + ( - \vec Q) = \vec P - \vec Q$
If $\vec P = \vec Q$then sum equal to zero.

Question: Given two vectors \[\overrightarrow A  = 3\hat i + \hat j + \hat k\] and \[\overrightarrow B  = \hat i - \hat j - \hat k\].  The area of the parallelogram whose two adjacent sides are represented by the vectors \[\;\;\overrightarrow A \,and\,\overrightarrow B \] is:
Calculate the area enclosed under the curve $f\left( x \right) = {x^2}$between the limits x = 2 and x = 3.
Solution:
$\int\limits_2^3 {f\left( x \right)dx = \int\limits_2^3 {{x^2}\,dx} } $
$ = \left. {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right|_2^3 = \frac{{{{\left( 3 \right)}^3}}}{3} - \frac{{{{\left( 2 \right)}^3}}}{3} = \frac{{19}}{3} = 6.333$

Question: Evaluate \[\int\limits_{ - \pi /4}^{\pi /4} {(1 + {{\sec }^2}\theta )d\theta } \]
The slopes of graph as shown in figure at point 1, 2 and 3 are m1, m2 and m3 respectively, then
The resultant of two forces $3P$ and $2P$is R. If the first force is doubled then the resultant is also doubled. The angle between the two forces is:
Solution:
${R^2} = {(3P)^2} + {(2P)^2} + 2 \times 3P \times 2P \times \cos \theta $ …(i)
${(2R)^2} = {(6P)^2} + {(2P)^2} + 2 \times 6P \times 2P \times \cos \theta $ …(ii)
by solving (i) and (ii), $\cos \theta = - \frac{1}{2}$ ⇒ $\theta = 120^\circ $

Question: Which of the following reactions is the metal displacement reaction? Choose the right option.
Options: Fe + 2HCl ⟶ FeCl2 + H2↑
2Pb(NO3)2 ⟶ 2PbO + 4NO2 + O2↑
Cr2O3 + 2Al \[\xrightarrow{\Delta }\] Al2O3 + 2Cr
Solution:
Metal displacement reactions are the ones in which more reactive metal displaces a less reactive metal from its compound. Thus, out of the given reactions, the metal displacement reaction is:
Cr2O3 + 2Al \[\xrightarrow{\Delta }\] Al2O3 + 2Cr

Both Assertion and Reason are true, but the Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is true, but Reason is false.
Both Assertion and Reason are false.
Solution: Law of parallelogram is one of the laws of vector addition which is used to find the resultant of two vectors. Component of a vector is a part of a given vector so it should be smaller or equal to the given vector.

Statement II: Amoeboid protozoans move and capture prey by putting out pseudopodia
Statement III: Plasmodium is a ciliated protozoan that causes malaria
Statement IV: Trypanosoma is a flagellated protozoan that causes sleeping sickness
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: All not some protozoans are heterotrophs and live as predators or parasites
Statement III is incorrect: Plasmodium is a sporozoan, not a ciliated protozoan. Paramecium is an example of a ciliated protozoan.

Question:
Assertion (A): Kingdom Protista has brought together organisms which, in earlier classifications, were placed in different kingdoms
Reason (R): The boundaries of Kingdom Protista are not well-defined
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are correct. However, the lack of well-defined boundaries (R) is a result of different biological perspectives, but it does not specifically explain why the kingdom was created to house previously separated organisms (A)

Question:
Assertion (A): Euglenoids have a rigid cell wall made of cellulose
Reason (R): Euglenoids possess a protein-rich layer called a pellicle which makes their body flexible
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is false: Euglenoids lack a cell wall.

Question:

Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Basidiomycetes include mushrooms, bracket fungi, and puffballs

Question:

Question:

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are correct facts from NCERT. However, the chemical composition of the cell wall (R) is not the reason why fungi prefer specific climatic conditions (A).

Question:

Reason (R): Viruses contain both RNA and DNA as their genetic material

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is false
Solution:
Assertion is false: Viruses are non-cellular organisms having an inert crystalline structure outside the living cell
Reason is false: A virus contains either RNA or DNA, never both.

Question:

Question:

Question:

Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements I and II are correct

Statements I, II, and IV are correct

Question:

Question:

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false

Solution: Both statements are true. However, the general susceptibility of enzymes to heat (R) does not explain the specific thermal stability mechanism of thermophilic enzymes (A).

Question:

Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement III is incorrect: Concanavalin A is a lectin, not an alkaloid.
Statement IV is incorrect: Morphine and codeine are alkaloids, while abrin and ricin are the listed toxins.

Question:

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Both are incorrect. Thermophilic enzymes are stable up 80◦C to 90◦C. Low temperature preserves enzymes in an inactive state; high temperature denatures them.

Question:

Question:
Assertion: Glucose and fructose give the same osazone.
Reason: During osazone formation stereochemistry at C1 and C2 is destroyed.
Options: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and the Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but the Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is true, but Reason is false.
Both Assertion and Reason are false.
Solution: Glucose and fructose are different from each other only at C1 and C2 positions (anomeric carbons). According to the mechanism, the first formed phenyl hydrazine undergoes rearrangement through a cyclic intermediate in which the secondary hydroxyl group at C2 becomes a ketone group and this ketone group then condenses with phenyl hydrazine to form the osazone.

Question:

Question:

Question:

Question: D-glucose and L-glucose are examples of:
Solution:
D-glucose is formed when glucose rotates the plane polarized light in the right direction (dextrorotation) and L-glucose is formed when glucose rotates the plane polarized light in the left direction (levorotation).
The D-glucose and L-glucose is non-superimposable mirror image of each other. Hense, D-glucose and L-glucose are examples of enantiomers.

Solution: Lactose, the disaccharide of milk, consists of galactose joined to glucose by a β-1,4-glycosidic linkage.

Lactose\[\beta - D\left( + \right)\]Glucose + \[\beta - D\left( + \right)\]Glucose(\[\beta - 1\], 4-glycosidic linkage)

Question: Glucose on oxidation with Tollen’s reagent and conc. HNO<sub>3</sub> produces respectively
Solution: Glucose undergoes oxidation with Fehling's solution or Tollen's reagent. Fehling's solution or Tollen's reagent is mild oxidizing agents. The product formed on oxidation is gluconic acid. Glucose is oxidized by nitric acid and produce saccharic acid or glucaric acid.

Question:

Reason: This is because free aldehyde group is present in pentaacetate.

Both Assertion and Reason are true, but the Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is true, but Reason is false.
Both Assertion and Reason are false.
Solution: In cyclic structure of glucose, the pentaacetate of glucose does not react with hydroxylamine indicating the absence of free \[ - CHO\] group.

Question: Which of the following statement is correct?

Gluconic acid is a dicarboxylic acid

Question:
Assertion: The process of hydrolysis of sucrose giving an equimolar mixture of glucose and fructose in the presence of acid or enzymes, is called inversion of sugar.
Reason: Sucrose is a disaccharide reducing sugar.
Options: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and the Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but the Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is true, but Reason is false.
Both Assertion and Reason are false.
Solution: Sucrose is dextrorotatory which on hydrolysis gives the equimolar mixture of dextrorotatory glucose and laevorotatory fructose. Since the laevorotation of fructose \[\left( { - {{92.4}^o}} \right)\] is more than dextrorotation of glucose \[\left( { + {{52.5}^o}} \right)\], the mixture is laevorotatory. Monosaccharides of sucrose are glucose and fructose. In the formation of sucrose, the reducing groups of glucose and fructose are involved in glycosidic bond formation, therefore, sucrose is a non-reducing sugar.

Question: Complete hydrolysis of starch gives :

galactose and fructose in equimolar amounts
glucose and galactose in equimolar amounts
Solution: Starch is a polymer of glucose.

Question:

Both Assertion and Reason are true, but the Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is true, but Reason is false.
Both Assertion and Reason are false.
Solution: The anomeric carbon can easily be distinguished from the other carbon atoms in the ring by fact that it is joined to two oxygen atoms.

Question:

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect

Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct

Statements II and IV are correct

Statement II is incorrect: ADA is crucial for the immune system to function

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: The ori is a sequence from where replication starts
Statement II: Any piece of DNA when linked to this sequence can be made to replicate within the host cells
Statement III: This sequence is also responsible for controlling the copy number of the linked DNA
Statement IV: For a vector to be effective, it should have very many recognition sites for a single restriction enzyme
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: The presence of more than one recognition site within the vector will generate several fragments, which will complicate the gene cloning.

Tags.Subject: Zoology
Tags.Topic: Tools of Recombinant DNA Technology - Cloning Vectors

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Stirred-tank bioreactors have been designed for the availability of oxygen throughout the bioreactor
Statement II: A bioreactor provides the optimal conditions for achieving the desired product
Statement III: The cells harbouring cloned genes of interest may be grown on a small scale in the laboratory or on large scale
Statement IV: If any protein encoding gene is expressed in a homologous host, it is called a recombinant protein
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: If any protein encoding gene is expressed in a heterologous host, it is called a recombinant protein

Question:
Assertion (A): Agrobacterium tumefaciens is widely used for transforming plant cells
Reason (R): It has a Ti-plasmid to transform normal plant cells into tumor cells
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are correct facts. However, the reason why we use it in biotechnology is that it has been disarmed (pathogenicity removed) to serve as a vector so as to deliver a piece of DNA (T-DNA) which is not mentioned in the Reason.
